During the Cold War, several leaders held power in the United States and the Soviet Union. In the United States, Presidents Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush Sr. were in charge at various times. In the Soviet Union, leaders such as Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, Andropov, Chernenko, and Gorbachev held power. These individuals shaped global politics through military, economic, and ideological strategies.
